walnut-cookies-teleculinaria.name: Walnut Cookies
instructions: Crush the nut kernel along with sugar, flour and bicarbonate. Then add the butter and mix well until you get a kind of thick sand. Add the egg and love it all.

Garnish trays with butter and dispose of marbles of the previous dough. Put over a half of walnut and put it in the oven, preheated at 180°C, for 25 minutes. Remove from the oven, take off the tray and serve.

warm-berry-compote.name: Warm Berry Compote
instructions: Stir frozen berries, sugar, orange juice, and orange zest together in a slow cooker. Cover and cook on High until bubbling, about 1 1/2 hours.
Stir cornstarch and water together in a cup until fully dissolved. Stir into berry mixture. Cover again and cook until thickened, 5 to 10 minutes. Serve warm or at room temperature.

watercress-salad-with-mustard-vinaigrett.name: Watercress Salad With Mustard Vinaigrette
instructions: Whisk mustard, vinegar, and garlic in a medium bowl; season with salt and pepper, then gradually stream in oil, whisking constantly. Whisk until dressing is emulsified and thick.

Add watercress to dressing, season with more salt and pepper, and gently toss until watercress is evenly coated (you want to be careful not to bruise or wilt greens when tossing, so use a light hand). Pile salad lightly onto plates.

watermelon-semifreddo-teleculinaria.name: Watermelon Semifreddo

ingredients.item: 120 g de farinha de trigo com fermento

instructions: Prepare the filling: cut the watermelon to pieces, eliminate the seeds, put it in a pot with sugar and ginger and bring it to the fire. Stir once in a while and lower the temperature as soon as it starts boiling. When it thickens, remove from heat and eliminate ginger and reserve.

Prepare the pasta: beat eggs with sugar for 5 minutes. Then wrap the flour, without knocking. Put on a tray with butter, line it with vegetable paper and unte again. Pour the dough into the tray and take it to the preheated oven at 180°C, about 10 minutes.

Remove from the oven and deform the pie on a sheet of vegetable paper, previously sprinkled with powdered sugar. Remove the paper and spread the stuffing, leaving a small margin around. Wrap the pie carefully and twist the ends of the vegetable paper to attach. Reserve.

Line a round shape 22 cm, removable background with adhesive film. Dispose of the cold pie paper, remove the ends and cut it into slices. Have the slices at the bottom of the shape and some at the sides.

Drain the gelatin leaves in cold water, drain and dissolve them in the hot soy. Beat the cream until it's firm, add the yogurt, the condensed milk and the jelly in yarn. Involve very well and pour the preparation into the form and take it to the refrigerator until solidify.
To deform, turn the shape backwards and remove the film. Decorate with watermelon and mint leaves and serve very cold.

white-bean-salad-with-pomegranate-and-pa.name: White Bean Salad With Pomegranate And Parsley

ingredients.prep: whites and pale greens only, cut on a diagonal

instructions: Toss beans, pomegranate seeds, lemon juice, oil, scallions, parsley, and ½ tsp. salt together in a medium bowl; season with salt and pepper.

why-is-it-so-good-gravy.name: Why-is-it-so-good Gravy

ingredients.prep: shiitake, or white button mushrooms, torn into large pieces

ingredients.item: Turkey stock or low-sodium chicken broth
. sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar

instructions: Preheat oven to 232°C. Toss wings in a large cast-iron skillet with 2 Tbsp. oil until coated. Roast, tossing halfway through, until golden brown and starting to crisp, 25–35 minutes.

Remove wings from oven and add garlic, shallots, ginger, mushrooms, peppercorns, and remaining 2 Tbsp. oil to pan; toss to coat. Return to oven and roast, tossing halfway through, until wings are deeply browned and crisp and vegetables are browned, 30–40 minutes more.
Meanwhile, mash together flour and butter in a small bowl with your fingers or a fork until homogenous. Set beurre manié aside.

Remove skillet from oven and set on stovetop over medium-high heat. Add sherry and cook, stirring and scraping up any browned bits with a wooden spoon, until sherry is almost completely reduced, about 2 minutes.

Scrape wings and aromatics into a medium pot and add parsley and stock. Bring to a low simmer over medium-high heat. Reduce heat and simmer gently, skimming fat from surface occasionally and turning wings so both sides get a chance to be submerged, until liquid is flavorful, mahogany in color, and reduced by about one-third, 30–40 minutes.

Fish out wings; discard. Pour mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large measuring glass; discard solids. Rinse out pot and pour turkey mixture back in. Bring to a simmer over medium. Whisking constantly, add reserved beurre manié and whisk until completely incorporated. Simmer, whisking often and skimming any foam from surface, until gravy is thick enough to coat a spoon, 6–8 minutes. Remove from heat and whisk in vinegar and MSG. Taste gravy and season with salt and pepper if desired.
DO AHEAD: Gravy (without vinegar and MSG) can be made 5 days ahead. Let cool, then cover and chill. Reheat over medium until simmering, then add vinegar and MSG.

winter-squash-bharta.name: Winter Squash Bharta

ingredients.item: . kashmiri chile powder or  0.5 tsp. cayenne pepper

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Using a sharp heavy knife, halve squash lengthwise and scoop out seeds with a spoon (if desired, clean and use the seeds in Smashed Green Beans). Rub cut sides of squash with 1 Tbsp. oil and season generously with salt. Place cut side down on a rimmed baking sheet and roast until tender, 60–75 minutes.

Scoop squash flesh from skin into a serving bowl. Using a potato masher or fork, smash until mostly smooth. Set aside a big spoonful of red onion and cilantro. Mix in chiles, ghee, coriander, cumin, chile powder, remaining red onion, remaining cilantro, and remaining 2 Tbsp. oil; season with salt and pepper.
Top squash with reserved onion and cilantro just before serving.
Do ahead: Squash can be baked and mashed 3 days ahead. Let cool; cover and chill.

*Most mustard oils are not FDA-approved as edible in the United States due to their high erucic acid content. If you are concerned, look for Yandilla, an FDA-approved brand, online, or use olive oil instead.

worlds-greatest-martini.name: World's Greatest Martini
instructions: Fill a cocktail shaker with fresh ice cubes.
Add about 1/2 ounce each dry and sweet vermouth.
Shake enough to coat ice and pour off liquid.
Add 8 ounces gin to coated ice in the shaker and shake vigorously.
Pour into 4 chilled martini glasses, garnish with pimiento-stuffed olive and serve.
